description: Execute a shell command. Sync mode (async=false): returns combined stdout+stderr output after command exits. Async mode (async=true): returns task_id immediately; poll with process_task_status for partial_output later. Timeout mode: if timeout is set and command doesn't finish within that duration, it auto-converts to async mode — returns task_id + partial output up to that point. For long-running servers (npm run dev, python -m http.server), ALWAYS use async=true to avoid timeout. Use cwd to specify a working directory (cross-platform, no need to prefix with 'cd /d'). Defaults to project root. Use env to pass custom environment variables (merged with current environment). Example: {"command": "npm run build", "cwd": "./frontend"}
name: execute_command

async.description: If true, returns immediately with task_id. Poll with process_task_status.

cwd.description: Working directory for the command (relative or absolute). Cross-platform - uses forward slashes. Default: project root.

env.description: Custom environment variables as key-value pairs. Merged with current process environment (user-provided values override inherited ones). Example: {"NODE_ENV": "production", "PORT": "3000"}

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds. When set, if the command doesn't finish within this duration, it auto-converts to async mode — returns task_id, partial_output up to that point, and a message. The process keeps running in background. Example: 30
